name: Butter Cookie Cups w. Chocolate.almond Filling
recipeIngredient: 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, sifted
6 tablespoons unsalted butter, cold, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
6 ounces semisweet chocolate, broken into pieces
recipeInstructions:
For cookies:
1. Blend flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor.
2. Cut in butter until the mixture coats the meal and forms coarse crumbs, using on/off turns.
3. Add egg yolk, vanilla, and almond extract. Process until the mixture gathers together.
4. Form dough into an 1-inch-diameter log on plastic wrap. Wrap tightly and refrigerate until well chilled, about 3 hours. (Can be prepared 2 days ahead.)
5. Set oven rack to lowest position and preheat to 350°F.
6. Butter 1 3/4 x 3/4 inch round tartlet pans.
7. Cut dough into scant 1/4-inch-thick slices. Press one slice into each pan.
8. Arrange cookie cups on a baking sheet.
9. Bake until golden brown, about 15 minutes.
10. Cool for 15 minutes on the rack.
11. Gently remove cookies from pans and cool completely on the rack. (Can be prepared 1 day ahead; store in an airtight container.)
For filling:
1. Using a fork, mash almond paste into cream in a heavy medium skillet over low heat until chocolate melts.
2. Remove from heat and mix in butter one tablespoon at a time until lukewarm.
3. Mix in amaretto liqueur and vanilla extract.
4. Cover the filling with a paper towel and plastic wrap. Refrigerate until just firm, about 1 hour.
5. Spoon filling into a pastry bag fitted with a medium star tip (no.6).
6. Pipe filling into crusts, about 3 inches above the rims.
7. Refrigerate at least 1 1/2 hours. (Can be prepared 6 hours ahead.) Serve chilled.
